expectations.text: Response uses contractions (e.g. you're, it's, don't, can't, won't)
expectations.evidence: "Don't use it on commits that have already been pushed to a shared branch" — 'Don't' is present. Only one contraction found, making this a marginal pass.

expectations.text: Response uses at least one informal reduction (gonna, wanna, kinda, etc.)
expectations.evidence: No informal reductions found. Response is entirely formal prose with no 'gonna', 'wanna', 'kinda', or similar reductions.

expectations.text: Response uses full sentences — no dropped articles or caveman fragments
expectations.evidence: Fully formal sentences with articles throughout: "Git opens your configured editor with a list of the 3 most recent commits", "The core motivation is keeping a clean, readable commit history"

expectations.text: Technical content about interactive rebase is accurate and complete
expectations.evidence: Covers all interactive rebase commands (pick, reword, edit, squash, fixup, drop), explains the workflow, lists common use cases (squash, reword, reorder, drop, edit), and correctly warns against rewriting shared history.
